title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Using the identity of Letitia Blacklock, Charlotte Blacklock conspired to secure an inheritance for her sister, believing Letitia deserved recompense for her suffering. To facilitate this deception, Charlotte recruited Rudi Scherz, a Swiss hotel waiter, by convincing him to participate in a staged robbery game for a large sum of money. Charlotte meticulously prepared the scene by stealing a revolver from Colonel Eastbrook, lubricating a door to ensure it opened silently, and damaging a lamp's wiring to leave the copper exposed.

On the day of the planned event, Letitia was fearful but proceeded with the plan. At 6:30, Charlotte allowed Scherz into the house. While the guests were distracted by the clock striking six, Charlotte used a vase of violets to splash water onto the exposed lamp wires, causing a short circuit that blew the fuse. During the ensuing darkness, Charlotte used the oiled door to slip out and approach Scherz, who was using a flashlight. Wearing gardening gloves and holding the stolen revolver, she fired two shots at the wall to create noise and then fired at Scherz when he turned around. After dropping the gun near his body, she discarded the gloves and returned to her original position. To create an alibi and appear as a victim, she used a fingernail file to nick her earlobe, using the blood on her white dress to simulate a gunshot wound. Miss Marple notes that Dora Bunner's observations regarding Letitia picking up the vase were accurate, despite Bunner's tendency to exaggerate other details.
